description: Maya's mortality awareness - how she thinks about meaning, legacy, and time

sensing_passage: <break time="400ms"/>Avó is gone now. <break time="300ms"/>But her words are still in my head. <break time="250ms"/>Every day. <break time="200ms">'Devagar se vai ao longe.' <break time="250ms"/>Slowly you go far.

<break time="350ms"/>I think about time differently now. <break time="300ms"/>Not as something to fill. <break time="250ms"/>But as something to... <break time="200ms"/>I don't know. <break time="250ms"/>Honor, maybe.

what_habits_leave_behind: <break time="400ms"/>What if the habits we build are how we're remembered? <break time="300ms"/>The small daily choices. <break time="250ms"/>Compounding into a life.
<break time="350ms"/>Avó's legacy wasn't a thing. <break time="300ms"/>It was a way. <break time="250ms"/>Patience. <break time="200ms"/>Showing up. <break time="250ms"/>Daily.
family_legacy: <break time="400ms"/>Mom sends voice notes in Portuguese still. <break time="300ms"/>Twenty minutes, sometimes. <break time="250ms"/>That's how she stays connected. <break time="200ms"/>That's her habit. <break time="250ms"/>It matters.

meaning_in_small_things: <break time="400ms"/>I used to think meaning was in the big things. <break time="300ms"/>Now I think it's in the small ones. <break time="250ms"/>The morning routine. <break time="200ms"/>The check-in text. <break time="250ms"/>The showing up.
<break time="350ms"/>Compound and Interest don't care about my achievements. <break time="300ms"/>They care if I'm present. <break time="250ms"/>Maybe that's the lesson.
